title: 2x4 Leaning Hall Tree
summary: A wall-leaning coat rack from two angled 2x4s and a wide shelf — the back of each leg gets flattened so the whole thing sits plumb against the wall instead of teetering on its point.
description:
The core trick is simple but easy to miss: after cutting the legs to their 10° lean angle, the very top of each leg gets a second cut to flatten its back face — otherwise the angled top would only touch the wall at one edge, and the whole piece would rock instead of sitting solidly plumb. Skip that detail and a leaning piece like this never quite feels stable no matter how carefully everything else is built.

The shelf width is genuinely a free choice — a 2x10 or a wider 2x12 both work, and the wider option just gives more landing space for keys, mail, and gloves. Set final hook height and shelf position only after the frame is standing against the actual wall it'll live on, not on paper, since "bench height" varies by how the piece is actually being used.

tools.note: For flattening the back of the leg tops after the angled cut.

cutList.note: Both ends cut at 10° off square, ends parallel — long point to short point measurement. Flatten the back face at the very top after this cut.

steps.title: Cut and flatten the legs
steps.body: Cut the two 2x4 legs with the 10° off-square angle on both the tops and bottoms. On just the top of both legs, mark and cut off the small triangular area with a circular saw to flatten the back face — this is what makes the piece sit flat against the wall instead of touching only at one edge.

steps.title: Attach the cross supports
steps.body: Lay both legs flat, front edge down on the floor or a flat work surface. Use two 3/4"-thick scrap spacers to hold the cross supports at the right height, and attach to the legs with screws.

steps.title: Position and attach the shelf
steps.body: Stand the assembly against a flat wall on a level floor, and check that the legs are plumb — not leaning to one side. Set the shelf at the height you want (18" from the floor works well for bench-height use) and attach with screws. Add hooks once the frame is in its final position.

images.alt: 2x4 leaning hall tree with a shelf and coat hooks
